_note: Living spec-clause index for tools/spec-coverage. Each clause: a spec pointer (file+section, kept in sync by tests/test_spec_coverage_registry.py) and a coverage status. `must_cover` = the CI-gated subset (a test tagged @pytest.mark.spec("<id>") must exist) — every clause a genuine test asserts. `coverage`=incidental/none clauses are tracked but omitted from the gate with an `omit_reason` (happy-path-only, cross-cutting principle, or untested). A map of which spec behaviours have a dedicated test, not a quality score.

clauses.title: photos-23 summarizes automatic/no-op GPS per destination; file paths appear only for user-review or blocker items
photos-24 re-derives every automatic GPS decision deterministically from its own validated inputs, never from the photos-23 summary
A held-back fingerprint-mismatch is confirmed on a later run only when the operator sets accept_fingerprint_change:true
At a formal decision stage the numbered JSON artifact is always produced, even if the result is complete/no-op
A native-GPS photo yields a time-anchor proposal only if close enough to a GPX point or short segment under configured thresholds
All numbered geotag artifacts are written flat into the .photos-ingest/ control directory
clauses.omit_reason: tests read/write artifacts at .photos-ingest paths but none assert the location as the behavior under test

clauses.title: Geotag artifacts must never be written inside 6-photos-by-dest or any scanned media folder
All numbered geotag artifacts are grouped by/cover destinations separately and are inspectable per destination
Each metadata write/rename is individually atomic (temp+atomic-rename or safe-write) so an interruption never leaves a corrupt intermediate
clauses.omit_reason: _exiftool_write tests check the tmp-unlink/original-intact path but no test simulates a mid-write interruption asserting atomicity

clauses.title: Automated GPS is never rolled back: no pre-state stored, no revert op emitted; it is recomputed from current inputs
A proposal may be auto-resolved without an explicit user_decision only when the governing config flag for its class opts in
A bucket is resolved from its own date's evidence or the destination timezone with no inheritance step; a manual offset applies only to that day
Each bucket's proposed offset is chosen in priority order: self-anchored, else timezone-derived, else blank manual-required
If 5-photos-by-date still contains photos, geotag must block before creating any geotag JSON artifact
Symlinks under by-dest (file or nested directory) are barred; geotag never follows a link and relies on prep's symlink guard for nested links
clauses.omit_reason: nested by-dest symlinks are delegated to prep's symlink guard (already tested); geotag has no independent nested-by-dest symlink code path

clauses.title: A video-class file under by-dest hard-stops geotag (videos belong in 4-videos-by-date), never silently skipped
by-dest must contain only image/raw photo files; any other-class non-media file hard-stops before any JSON, reporting offending paths
Geotag verifies schema/cache versions via the handoff's recorded fingerprints, not by opening and inspecting prep's database directly
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts geotag avoids opening prep's DB; would need a new test

clauses.title: Rename planning detects case-insensitive conflicts and rejects unsafe rename plans
Changing a manual GPS value overwrites to the new value while the pinned original stays unchanged for later full withdrawal
A candidate anchors only if its GPX timestamp is within the plausible-clock-error window of the frame's naive time read as UTC, applied during the spatial search
Once time decisions are complete, resolved UTC is computed for every media file and persisted into SQLite
Per-file work may run concurrently under -j but concurrency must never change semantic results
A config or camera-group-classification change restales photos-21 and forces recompute/revalidate of time requirements
clauses.omit_reason: photos-21 records the time-policy fingerprint dependency but no test mutates config/classification and asserts photos-21 restales

clauses.title: Conflicting anchor candidates (outside the chosen cluster) trigger user review
The largest agreeing offset cluster wins (within spread, never averaged), with point-before-segment then closest as the within-cluster tiebreak
A folder holding only sub-destinations is materialized as a file_less container destination for downward decision propagation
Container destination cells never block and never demand input; each auto-resolves by inheritance yet stays overridable
A container destination carries no clock-offset cells (offsets neither cascade nor roll up)
Artifacts are generated with all human-decision fields already present and empty unless previously filled
Every durable artifact validates upstream, records dependencies, and rejects downstream use if dependencies changed
A destination is the folder directly containing media; nested media-holding folders are their own separate destinations
Results are merged in deterministic path-sorted order so journal, totals, resume facts, and mismatches are identical regardless of job count
The development-subfolder presence check is re-evaluated at every invocation (plan, execute, finalize) and hard-stops before any mutation or bundling
The development-subfolder check is strict: mere presence triggers the hard-stop even if the folder is empty, before any JSON artifact
A ledger entry for a file that no longer exists is kept (not pruned), triggers no operation, and is carried into the archived DB
Authored drift confirmations are preserved across reruns; a confirmation whose bucket no longer triggers is stale-flagged
corrected_offset_seconds is sanity-validated (numeric, within +/-86400); out-of-range leaves the artifact unchanged
While photos-22 requires_user_input the workflow stops and does not create photos-23 or photos-24
The GPS-drift validation gate runs between resolved UTC and GPS planning, consuming complete photos-21 and current-offset resolved UTC
The validated drift offset lives in photos-22 and does not mutate the photos-21 decision it refines
Once photos-22 is complete, resolved UTC is recomputed with each confirmed validated offset, then the SQLite cache and fingerprint are persisted
A bucket is a drift review item iff its offset source is manual/timezone (not GPX self-anchor), it has no native-GPS anchor, and GPX coverage exists
An untouched drift bucket blocks the gate; a zero scrub is never implied by inaction (confirmed:false => requires_user_input)
The plan records photos-22 as a direct dependency so execute rejects a plan whose drift confirmation was withdrawn after planning
Execution must not invent new rename decisions or choose a different suffix; it only applies renames present in photos-24
Execution must not recalculate decisions; it only validates and applies decisions recorded in the executable plan
Each rename is re-verified no-clobber at execute time; an unexpectedly occupied target is a blocker, never a clobber
Execution is allowed only after photos-24 exists, is current, and validates all upstream dependencies
Folder fallback proposal is inherited from the nearest ancestor destination as a confirmable proposal, else manual-required
A file placed from the folder fallback is a manual placement (marker manual_fallback), reversible via the pre-state ledger
Folder fallback is optional and never blocks (requires_user_input always false); un-located photos fall through to remaining Section 23 options
clauses.omit_reason: fallback cells are exercised but no test asserts requires_user_input is always false / that un-located photos fall through

clauses.title: Folder fallback inheritance is recursive parent-first, parent to child only; an authored fallback re-roots the chain
Folder GPS fallback ranks after preserve-native/manual-lock/GPX and before accept-unlocated/block in the GPS resolution order
A geotag JSON existing on disk does not by itself mean execution is allowed
Changing the filename format invalidates the executable rename plan
Filename timestamp format is config-driven via filename_timestamp_format and must not be hard-coded
Filename-format config participates in dependency fingerprints and the extension follows the project's normalization rules
The filename timestamp is computed from resolved UTC converted to destination civil timezone, not raw camera time and not UTC text
Finalize generates photos-26-complete-log.json carrying prep's log forward as a superset, not re-deriving or discarding prep history
Finalize captures the photos-26-geotag-ingest.db backup as a consistent read-only copy of the live DB without mutating it
Finalize is a separate explicitly-invoked command, not part of execute and not run automatically
clauses.omit_reason: finalize is invoked as its own subcommand in tests but none assert that execute does not auto-finalize

clauses.title: Finalize writes a self-describing manifest with the SHA-256 of each bundled item, including DB snapshots
Geotag and finalize never perform the merge; merge requires a successfully finalized workspace
Finalize is non-destructive: it reads and bundles, never mutating the workspace, artifacts, live SQLite DB, or library
Finalize requires geotag to have ended successfully (complete executed plan + summary) and refuses an incomplete or stale state
photos-21-time-decisions.json is the first durable geotag artifact; no earlier geotag JSON is produced
Each downstream artifact must include its full flattened dependency set, not only the immediately previous artifact's recursive block
GPS planning must not begin until every file has valid resolved UTC
GPX folder parsing/fingerprinting (disk-heavy) runs only after in-memory camera-group recognition passes
GPX folder scanning/parsing/fingerprinting must happen before the time-decision stage
clauses.omit_reason: GPX-anchored offset inference implies GPX is loaded first, but no test asserts the stage ordering

clauses.title: The current GPX fingerprint is recorded as a dependency unconditionally whenever a GPX set is loaded (conservative over-inclusion)
clauses.omit_reason: dep blocks carry gpx_fingerprint but no test asserts it is recorded even for an artifact that used no GPX evidence

clauses.title: If GPX is unavailable or disabled, later artifacts record that no GPX evidence was used
clauses.omit_reason: GPXIndex status disabled/missing is tested but no test asserts downstream artifacts record the absence

clauses.title: The prep handoff is verified by recomputed content_fingerprint, not its whole-file byte hash, so a run-only re-prep does not restale geotag
If camera-group classification is incomplete, geotag does not create photos-21, prints snippets, and stops requiring config update + rerun
A missing managed 0-6 folder hard-stops geotag (restore + re-run prep); geotag never creates or repairs the structure
After preflight, in-memory file objects are created for files under 6-photos-by-dest only
An invalid user value is preserved and flagged for correction, never silently deleted, coerced, or repaired
Execution journals every metadata write, marker write, move, or rename and updates SQLite/cache after successful writes
A JSON-artifact dependency must name the file and store the SHA-256 of its exact file bytes
The pre-state ledger is per-workspace; a fresh workspace starts with no ledger and pins whatever GPS files now carry as its original
clauses.omit_reason: ledger is per-workspace by construction but no test covers the fresh-workspace re-pin semantics; would need a new test

clauses.title: The workspace lock is acquired at startup before preflight; if another run holds it geotag exits fail-fast without scanning, planning, or writing
A manual GPS override is reversible via the pinned pre-state; withdrawing undoes its effect and changing re-applies cleanly
A misplaced root entry (loose file incl. dotfiles, non-managed folder, or symlink) hard-stops geotag strictly
Geotag never plans or applies time/GPS metadata or renames for a video and never reasons about videos in its machinery
The tool never mutates autonomously; the executor acts only on decisions the user wrote into decision fields
clauses.omit_reason: broad principle; the executor acts only on plan ops derived from decisions but no single test isolates this obligation

clauses.title: Planning never assumes a currently occupied filename will free up via a planned rename; existing disk files are treated as permanently occupied during suffix allocation
The operation set is fixed single-threaded before concurrent work; execution never decides anything concurrently
clauses.omit_reason: j1/jN equality implies it, but no geotag test asserts the operation set is fixed before the concurrent apply

clauses.title: JSON artifacts must not be produced for preliminary reports, blocked preconditions, or config snippets
The user fills existing fields or changes boolean/string values only; never adds structure, brackets, keys, or sections
clauses.omit_reason: a UX contract; tests fill existing fields but none assert structure need not be authored

clauses.title: Staleness must not rely only on mtime, size, cached metadata, recursive dependency blocks, or previous validation results
Partial or failed worker output is never journaled as a confirmed operation or cached as a valid fingerprint
The workflow must not preserve outdated automated GPS decisions merely to avoid touching media files
clauses.omit_reason: no test forces an upstream change and asserts stale automated GPS is re-derived rather than preserved

clauses.title: A destination/proposal with no proposal at all keeps requires_user_input true (conservative default)
If any dependency does not match, the artifact is stale and execution must not proceed from stale dependency state
Resolved UTC must not be finalized for files in a destination whose timezone/time dependencies are incomplete
Each (group, destination) offset cell is inferred only from that group's native-GPS frames in that destination
Clock offsets are never inherited from an ancestor destination (unlike timezone and folder GPS fallback)
There is no upward roll-up: a parent's offset is never computed by aggregating its children's cells
An offset cell splits into per-naive-date buckets when a (group, destination) spans more than one day; a midnight crossing splits in two
Camera clock offset is inferred/applied per (camera group, destination[, day]), never once per camera globally
Workflow order is strictly time first, GPS second, rename/metadata execution last
photos-24 must include a flattened dependency list directly naming and fingerprinting all required upstream artifacts and inputs (handoff by content_fingerprint)
An executable plan is produced only when photos-21/22/23 are complete, UTC is current, renames are safe, no blockers remain, and all dependencies are current
The executable plan includes a no-clobber rename only where the corrected local-time filename differs from the current name
After each metadata write the decoded-content fingerprint is verified unchanged; a mismatch holds the operation back, never silently accepted
A prep run must occur after the most recent by-date to by-dest move before geotag runs (hard contract requirement)
On regeneration, existing user-filled decision fields are preserved where the logical target is unchanged and never silently overwritten
apply_manual only targets files with no native GPS (preserve-native wins), so the captured pre-state is in practice always the absent sentinel
For a manual GPS override the pinned pre-state is committed to the ledger before the GPS write that overwrites it
On first application a manual GPS write captures and pins the file's prior GPS (or an absent sentinel) in the ledger, keyed by content fingerprint
The pinned pre-state is the true original, written once and never overwritten by subsequent runs of the same or changed decision
The workflow recalculates only the affected downstream artifacts when upstream inputs change; a no-op run is a no-op
Machine-generated proposal/status fields may be recalculated on regeneration
clauses.omit_reason: rerun tests recompute proposals while preserving decisions but none isolate proposal recalculation as the assertion

clauses.title: Time/filename recomputation rewrites timestamp and filename in place within by-dest and never moves the file to another destination or location
Before an artifact is used, each named JSON dependency must be re-read and re-hashed and must match the recorded hash
Execution re-hashes JSON deps, validates non-JSON deps, and rejects stale plans before any mutation
Re-running a fully-applied plan is a no-op; execution never reapplies a present write/rename and never chooses a different suffix
Geotag is re-runnable after a successful execute (not a terminal run-once phase), until the workspace is merged/sealed
Residual content in 1-strays/2-missing-metadata/3-redundant-jpgs/4-videos-by-date is tolerated and does not block geotag
clauses.omit_reason: no test places residuals in those folders and asserts geotag still passes; would need a new test

clauses.title: Resolved UTC uses the file's (group, destination, date) effective offset plus the destination effective timezone; a file is solvable only when both are complete
On resume execution re-derives each file's actual state: target present skip, source+matching fingerprint re-apply idempotently, differing fingerprint blocks
Execution is safely re-runnable after a crash/partial application, re-validating all dependencies on re-run
Every human-authored value (config it reads + decision fields) is sanity-validated before use, in addition to hash checks
Geotag operates only on files under 6-photos-by-dest and never geotags files still in 0-5 folders
On a sealed/terminal merge marker geotag hard-stops immediately, mutating nothing and touching nothing, directing to a fresh workspace
On a sealed workspace with files at root/0-sources, geotag warns of a likely new dump and leaves the dump exactly where it is
The journal and SQLite (incl. pre-state captures) go through a single controlled writer; the pre-state capture pass completes before the concurrent write pass
On resume, already-applied or already-satisfied target states are detected and skipped; only remaining operations are applied
The pre-mutation snapshot is labelled for the geotag phase so it never name-collides with a prep snapshot for the same plan id
With snapshots_required, execution aborts before any mutation if a required pre-mutation snapshot fails (otherwise snapshots are optional)
A non-empty 0-sources means an un-processed dump waiting; geotag hard-stops directing the user to re-run prep
A user decision that can no longer be safely applied is preserved and marked stale/requiring-review, never deleted
If prep/by-dest validation fails, geotag prints a stale-state report and produces no JSON artifact
No-clobber suffix is deterministic: no suffix if base free, first collision -001, second -002, etc.
photos-25 records artifact/run identity, the plan SHA + flattened upstream chain it summarizes, per-dest+global counts, resume facts, failures, status, mismatches, and separate run metadata
photos-25 is terminal and is never re-hashed as an upstream dependency
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts photos-25 is never re-hashed/depended-on; accept_fingerprint_change read-back is the only consumption tested

clauses.title: Final status is success/partial/failed/rejected; a non-empty fingerprint-mismatch list forces partial, and a stale-plan rejection writes no summary at all
Unrecorded by-dest media or missing by-date photos hard-stop geotag with a targeted re-run-prep blocker, not a generic stale message
The plan distinguishes three GPS operation origins (apply manual, revert manual, recompute automated) and the transformation log records which applied
Destination timezone auto-resolves by default (nested geography) without per-destination confirmation, staying overridable
Timezone-derived (and manual) offset proposals are confirmable-only and never auto-applied; only a GPX self-anchor auto-resolves under policy flags
photos-21 must settle the civil timezone for every destination under by-dest, with pre-created decision fields
Timezone inheritance is recursive parent-first, flowing parent to child only, never sibling to sibling
Timezone proposal priority is self-proposed, then nearest-ancestor inherited, then config default, else blank
A manual timezone set at a folder re-roots the inheritance chain there for its descendants
clauses.omit_reason: manual-override-wins is tested, but no test shows a manual timezone re-rooting the chain inherited by descendants

clauses.title: A destination with no self/ancestor/config-default timezone keeps an empty manual field and is marked requires_user_input
An exiftool failure is recorded and leaves the file at pre-operation state; it is never recorded as applied or treated as a no-op next run
Carried-forward fingerprints are verified transitively at the owning step, not redundantly recomputed, but a change still surfaces downstream
clauses.omit_reason: flattened deps surface changes (tested) but the transitive/no-redundant-recompute nuance has no dedicated test

clauses.title: A no-anchor bucket with a resolved timezone gets offset = -(civil tz UTC offset at the bucket's earliest naive instant), computed DST-aware
On an uninitialized workspace (no root sentinel) geotag hard-stops with run-prep-first and creates nothing
Unknown camera groups produce directly pasteable, valid config snippets as textual output (not JSON artifacts)
The workflow produces user-facing outputs at each blocked/decision point so the user always knows stage, why blocked, which artifact, and next action
clauses.omit_reason: many tests assert specific messages but none cover the full user-output contract as a unit

clauses.title: The resolved-UTC cache is created only after validating all upstream dependencies including photos-21
The resolved-UTC cache exposes a deterministic SHA-256 fingerprint over per-file rows plus input fingerprints, stable across unchanged runs
A config edit outside the time-policy subset must not restale already-resolved UTC; the whole-config hash is integrity not the staleness trigger
Sanity-validation runs whenever a value is consumed on a run, never cached as permanently valid
clauses.omit_reason: validation is exercised per run but no test asserts it is not cached as permanently valid across runs

clauses.title: A sanity-validation failure is a hard blocker located precisely; geotag produces no downstream artifact and mutates nothing
The clock-error window is timezone-independent; the destination timezone must not be used to bound matching
Withdrawing a manual GPS decision emits an explicit revert operation driving the field to the pinned pre-state (restore coords or clear)
Execution writes photos-25-execution-summary.json and produces a final execution summary
move between dests re-evaluates under the new dest tz; old values not carried
geotag never records a move: reprep gate blocks, handoff/by-dest untouched
withdraw reverts GPS only; no time/name revert, never relocates across dests
-j1 vs -jN produce identical summary + file tree (geotag and merge)
journal writes originate only on the main thread (geotag and merge)
symlink under 6-photos-by-dest blocks the run and is never followed
Suffix allocation treats existing library names and all incoming batch files as occupied
Any misplaced workspace-root entry blocks: loose file, non-managed folder, or symlink
An un-mergeable file is left in by-dest (never deleted) and the run ends partial without sealing
Library-file fingerprints are computed on the fly and cached in workspace SQLite, at most once while unchanged
A collision is detected per target by a single existence check (no library-wide index)
clauses.omit_reason: plan tests exercise the per-target check but none asserts the single-existence-check mechanism / absence of a wide index

clauses.title: Results aggregate deterministically so two safe job counts produce the same summary content
The library lock is held throughout the concurrent move pass; concurrency never relaxes cross-run exclusion
merge plan/dry-run/execute leave photos-00-config.json byte-identical
bad merge policy -> blocker, no plan, no summary, no placement
Merge creates any missing destination subdirectory under library_root when placing a file
A re-run reproduces the same targets and anti-collision names; never re-allocates a placed file's suffix
Different-content collision renames the INCOMING file; the library file is authoritative and untouched
With no saved plan, dry-run stops and directs the operator to run plan first
Dry-run validates the real saved plan and reports a concise summary, never a simulation or full dump
On success execute captures photos-35-merge-ingest.db, a consistent copy of the live ingest DB
Execute re-confirms each target free before placing; never places onto an occupied target, never renames/overwrites a library file
Execute writes no SQLite rows; the only SQLite write is the planning-time fingerprint cache
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts execute adds zero DB rows; would need a row-count/before-after DB check

clauses.title: execute applies the saved photos-30 disposition verbatim, never re-derives
TOCTOU clobber: target occupied by different content at execute -> blocker, source kept, library byte unchanged
A planned-free target occupied at execute by identical content is treated as already-in-library: remove source, no write
Execution revalidates the plan before any mutation and rejects stale/missing/unverifiable/wrong-tool plans
The plan's safe-alternative name is used only where the plan recorded it for an anticipated collision
A merge that fails to acquire either lock, or finds the marker absent, exits having mutated nothing
A finalized entry whose file is absent from by-dest is not a blocker (already-merged/resume case)
clauses.omit_reason: resume tests move files out so the entry-absent path is exercised, but no test asserts absence-is-not-a-blocker

clauses.title: A free target places directly with no library read; fingerprinting happens only on a collision
clauses.omit_reason: plan_placed_new exercises a free target but does not assert that the library file was never read/fingerprinted

clauses.title: Merge holds both the workspace and library-side locks for the duration of plan and execute
init-library is the only path that creates the .photos-library marker, no-clobber; second run is a no-op success
init-library inside a workspace with no path reads library_root from config and blesses it (no config write)
init-library inside a workspace with an explicit path blesses the dir and writes only library_root into config
init-library outside any workspace with no path is an error (nothing to bless, no config)
init-library outside any workspace with an explicit path blesses the dir only
An interrupted (not fully successful) merge is not sealed and resumes normally
after a partial run the journal holds only confirmed moves
A library file is never deleted, overwritten, renamed, or its organization altered; read only to fingerprint on a collision
The library lock is a .photos-merge.lock dotfile keyed to library_root, serializing merges across workspaces
Order is workspace lock, then library_root validation + marker check, then library lock
photos-35-merge-log.json is photos-26 copied forward with a per-file merge step appended, deterministically
Merge maps each by-dest destination to a library subpath preserving the curated structure (identity default)
The destination-to-library mapping is deterministic: same by-dest file maps to the same target every run
marker validated before the library lock: marker-less lib drops no .photos-merge.lock
The move is copy-to-temp, verify, atomic-rename-into-target, then remove source last, never losing a file
Merge moves each successfully-placed photo into the library and removes it from by-dest (success empties by-dest of photos)
snapshot + revalidation precede the parallel move pass
A never-merged workspace has no photos-35 log; photos-26 remains its authoritative record
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts absence of photos-35 / photos-26 authority on a never-merged workspace

clauses.title: The workspace never keeps a duplicate of a file already in the library
Nothing about the library's state is persisted library-side except the lock
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts that merge persists no library-side state beyond the lock

clauses.title: Merge builds no full library index and performs no library-wide deduplication
clauses.omit_reason: absence of an index/dedup is not asserted by any test; would need a bounded-by-batch check

clauses.title: Merge never mutates by-dest content or a file's name except a library-collision incoming rename
stale-plan rejection writes no photos-31 summary at all
merge placement leaves EXIF time/GPS byte-identical (both placement primitives)
Merge does not tear down the workspace or clear leftovers
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts merge leaves workspace/leftovers in place beyond by-dest photos

clauses.title: Merge does not follow a symlink into or out of the managed tree
Merge does not perform the jpg/tif development breakout
A no-op re-prep refreshing only run metadata does not trip the post-finalize currency check
clauses.omit_reason: no test exercises a metadata-only re-prep passing the content-fingerprint currency check

clauses.title: Merge does not re-organize, re-deduplicate, or re-fingerprint by-dest content (prep's fingerprints reused)
The by-dest enumeration is built from the finalized record (handoff joined to photos-24 renames by fingerprint), not a disk scan
Per-file preconditions record the reused by-dest content fingerprint plus the file's current size/mtime
clauses.omit_reason: plan tests assert the content_fingerprint precondition but not the current size/mtime stat

clauses.title: plan mutates nothing: by-dest + library byte-identical, no 31/35/journal/seal
Merge recomputes the handoff content_fingerprint and hard-stops 're-run geotag and re-finalize' on drift
preflight blocker -> no summary, library byte-unchanged
by-dest photo absent from finalized set -> re-run-prep blocker, no placement
Merge does not recompute the final name; the only change is the anti-collision incoming rename
After a failed (all-blocked) or rejected (pre-mutation) summary, re-planning is allowed (nothing moved)
Merge requires 0-sources empty, confirming no un-processed dump in the inbox
library_root must carry the .photos-library marker; missing marker hard-blocks 'run init-library first' and is never auto-created
Merge re-verifies by-dest is the clean photo-only set (no by-date photos, only image/raw, no dev subfolder), else blocks
Merge requires the workspace finalized (archival package + photos-26 log), else hard-stops 'run finalize first'
Merge requires an executed photos-24 plan with photos-25 status success, else blocks
Merge hard-stops 'run prep first' if the workspace is not initialized (root sentinel absent)
A missing managed 0-6 folder hard-stops merge (it never creates/repairs the structure)
On success merge automatically re-seals the archival package to include the merge log/summary/DB snapshot, rewriting manifest SHA-256s
Re-seal re-bundles current artifacts by reading them; it never re-runs geotag's finalize or re-derives its record
Residuals in 4-videos-by-date, 2-missing-metadata, 3-redundant-jpgs, 1-strays do not block merge
clauses.omit_reason: no test places residuals in those folders and asserts merge still passes preflight

clauses.title: The renamed-in/source-unlink-pending crash window is recognized and completed; no file lost or half-present
Resume re-derives each file's state from the filesystem plus its recorded content fingerprint
Resume is via execute; plan refuses while a prior merge is in flight but unsealed (partial/success summary + saved plan)
Same-content collision is recorded already_present: place/rename nothing, remove the by-dest source; re-run is a no-op on the library
The durable seal marker, not by-dest emptiness, is the authority for 'this workspace is finished'
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts the seal marker (vs emptiness) governs finished-state

clauses.title: Seal and re-seal happen only on full success; a partial/failed/crashed run writes no seal and stays re-runnable
The terminal seal marker is a separate photos-00-sealed.json alongside the guard, not a guard field
After sealing, every media-mutating script refuses on this workspace; prune-quarantine is the sole exception
If the workspace is sealed, merge hard-stops immediately touching nothing
merge sealed path warns but relocates nothing, library untouched
required pre-mutation snapshot fails -> abort before any placement, no seal
The by-dest source is removed only after its verified library copy is in place (last step), so a crash never loses a file
Anti-collision suffix is append-at-max+1 (not gap-filling), never reusing a previous number on a re-run
photos-31-merge-summary.json is written to .photos-ingest/ on finishing; never an upstream dependency, never re-hashed
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts the summary is non-dependency / never re-hashed

clauses.title: Counts partition the batch into placed-new/renamed/already-present/blocked; removed = batch - blocked
Final status is one of success, partial, failed, or rejected
The summary is grouped by destination with a global summary, separating timestamps from fingerprints
clauses.omit_reason: jobs-determinism test reads the per-destination grouping but does not assert the grouping/separation structure itself

clauses.title: The summary records per file: by-dest path, final library path, renamed-for-library, already-present, removed-from-by-dest
clauses.omit_reason: the per-file fields are read in jobs-determinism comparison but no test asserts each field's value against an expectation

clauses.title: The summary records the SHA-256 of photos-24/photos-25/handoff that pinned the merged state
clauses.omit_reason: full-success test checks geotag ids but not the merged{} sha256 block

clauses.title: Merge sweeps stale .tmp-xdev-*.part temps from targeted dirs at the start of execute and records the count
The step-1 temporary uses the recognizable deterministic name .tmp-xdev-*.part

clauses.title: A forbidden sidecar (.xmp/.dop/.pp3) is a hard block

clauses.title: no special re-import path from 2-missing-metadata

clauses.title: All media operations are no-clobber, planned and re-verified at execute time, performed atomically

clauses.title: Two safe job counts produce the same semantic plan and the same dependency fingerprints
Unchanged already-fresh files produce no operation and are reported as no_op / already_correct

Each numbered artifact is owned by one phase; only that phase writes it, and a re-run refreshes only its own artifacts, never another phase's
At the end of each phase a complete, self-sufficient audit record exists that stands alone if no later phase runs (absent later phase = 'not advanced', never 'incomplete')

clauses.title: Every mutation traces to a recorded human decision or a deterministic rule on recorded inputs, never an autonomous tool choice

clauses.title: A DB snapshot is written to a temp then atomically renamed (clobber-on-refresh of the same-phase snapshot), never leaving a corrupt file
A DB snapshot must be a transactionally consistent image (online-backup/VACUUM INTO), not a raw byte-copy of a possibly-open file
Each phase captures one retained DB backup snapshot at its successful end; re-running refreshes only that phase's snapshot
A phase that does not complete successfully writes no new snapshot, leaving the prior one intact
Validate upstream -> create artifact -> record dependencies -> reject downstream use if dependencies changed

Prep/geotag pick the first-free name (bare-first); merge appends at max+1 (append-only, reproducible)

clauses.title: Prep's timestamp is from the raw camera-naive source, provisional, not corrected/timezone-resolved/UTC-converted

If gpx_root resolves inside the managed tree, prep must skip that subtree during scanning so GPX is never organized

A file is moved/renamed/metadata-written only on a real difference; an already-correct file produces a no-op

Only artifacts whose dependency fingerprints depend on a changed input are regenerated; unaffected artifacts left as-is
Work already done and still valid is reused, not recomputed; expensive ops run only for genuinely new/changed inputs
Library identity is a single empty .photos-library marker created once by init-library; the data path only reads it, hard-stopping if absent
The library is never rescanned wholesale and never mutated except by additive no-clobber placement; no library-wide index, dedup, or renaming
library_root is validated like every configured path and must be outside the workspace and its managed 0-6 tree
Lock acquired at startup before any planning/scanning/fingerprinting/metadata-extraction/dry-run/execution
the whole-run lock covers execute, not just plan
Every command that reads or touches the workspace acquires the single workspace lock
clauses.omit_reason: lock contention tested per command; no single test asserts every workspace-touching command takes the lock

clauses.title: lock-contended plan produces no plan artifact and no journal
At most one pipeline invocation may run against a workspace at a time (total mutual exclusion)
Lock is released on both normal exit and error
A stale lock from a crashed process is detectable (owner identity/liveness/start time) and recoverable
Stale-lock takeover is conservative: only a lock whose owner is provably gone may be reclaimed
clauses.omit_reason: flock-based locking; the OS releases the lock on process death, so there is no stale-reclaim code path to exercise (conservative by construction)

clauses.title: The transformation log is one file per phase, each produced by copying the predecessor forward and extending; each is a strict superset
Each transformation that followed an authored decision references that decision so the why is attached to the what
A photo's log entry accumulates steps as it advances; each phase only appends its own steps and never rewrites earlier ones
A phase fails or warns/produces a clearly-partial record if retained history is insufficient, never silently emitting a last-run-only log
The log is pretty-printed, stably/deterministically ordered, human-legible JSON that is itself the readable artifact
The log is keyed by content fingerprint (surviving rename/move/EXIF write); other-class files are not fingerprinted or logged as journeys
The log records manual-GPS apply/change/withdraw and the pinned pre-state captured or restored
Records the log is built from are retained across runs to reconstruct each file's per-file history; per-run journals not discarded/truncated
Starting jpg/tif development inside the workspace before geotag is settled is a hard-stop
Finalize is required before merge
Geotag proceeds only when by-date has no photos, by-dest is photo-only, no dev subfolder, and by-dest is prep-consistent
Geotag rerun is safe: preserved human decisions, already-placed GPS no-op, already-finalized renames recompute to the same name
Geotag is freely re-runnable any number of times (including after a prior successful execution) until the workspace is merged
clauses.omit_reason: rerun behavior exercised; no dedicated re-runnable-until-merged assertion beyond rerun-safe

clauses.title: New photos never reach geotag behind prep's back; files prep has not recognized are not in the hashed handoff
Re-prep is mandatory after the latest by-date->by-dest move (stat-only), carrying cache identity forward
A rerun does exactly as much work as changed inputs require; an upstream change may pull affected artifacts back into the decision loop
clauses.omit_reason: covered conceptually by staleness tests; no dedicated assertion that a rerun resurfaces only affected decisions

clauses.title: The user moves PHOTOS ONLY into 6-photos-by-dest; by-dest stays photo-only (no non-media, no videos)
A file that cannot be merged is left in by-dest and keeps the run partial WITHOUT sealing, so the operator can resolve and re-run
On a collision with differing fingerprints, merge gives the incoming file a safe differentiating name and places it, leaving the library file untouched
On a collision with equal fingerprints, merge writes/renames nothing in the library, removes the by-dest source, records already_present
A library file's fingerprint is computed on the fly at collision time and cached so it is never fingerprinted twice; by-dest reuses prep's cache
Merge is idempotent/resumable: a re-run recognizes already-merged files, completes interrupted removal, applies only outstanding moves
Merge takes a library-side lock keyed to library_root in addition to the workspace lock, so no two merges write the same library concurrently
The library lock is fail-fast and stale-detectable; a merge that cannot take it exits without touching the library
Library files are never renamed and never overwritten by merge; on a collision the incoming by-dest file is renamed, never the library file
Merge confirms the .photos-library marker before acquiring the lock, so no .photos-merge.lock is dropped into a non-library directory
Per-file move is copy-to-temp -> verify -> atomic rename into verified-free target -> only THEN remove the by-dest source as the last journaled step
Merge moves each placed photo, removing it from by-dest; a full success leaves by-dest empty; already-present files also removed
Merge requires geotag ended successfully and the workspace was finalized (archival package + photos-26 log exist)
Merge writes photos-31-merge-summary.json recording per-file/total paths, renames, already-present, removed, and blockers (terminal record)
Merge re-seals by bundling now-current artifacts as a read; it does not re-run geotag's finalize logic or re-derive geotag's portion
On successful merge the archival package is re-sealed automatically (re-bundled with merge log/summary/DB snapshot and refreshed manifest)
Merge re-validates the finalized plan is current with present by-dest and hard-stops if a by-dest photo postdates finalize
Each phase's plan/decision artifact lives at a fixed canonical control-dir path; plan writes it there, dry-run/execute read it there
A validate/apply command that finds the canonical plan missing stops and directs the operator to plan first
No command takes a path flag for where to put or find its plan; the canonical location is the contract
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts the absence of a plan path flag in the CLI

clauses.title: A planning command prints the path it saved to
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts the planning command prints the saved path

clauses.title: Re-planning never clobbers a prior plan/decision file: when content differs the existing artifact is renamed aside under -NNN and announced
A byte-identical re-run writes nothing and backs up nothing, so the decision loop accumulates no redundant backups
clauses.omit_reason: no test asserts a byte-identical re-plan writes/backs-up nothing

clauses.title: An empty by-dest is NOT a reliable clean-slate signal; the durable terminal/sealed marker, not folder state, is the authority
The seal is a separate photos-00-sealed.json alongside the guard (recording merged/run-id/library_root), not a field in the prep-owned guard
sealed-workspace new-dump path warns but leaves the file exactly in place
A partial merge (blockers) or a failed/crashed run writes no seal marker, so the workspace stays open for re-run
prune-quarantine is the sole op permitted on a sealed workspace
Every media-mutating script checks the seal marker at startup and on its presence hard-stops, touching nothing
Merge reaches success only when every by-dest photo is moved/confirmed and no un-merged photo remains; on success the workspace is sealed
Snapshots are disabled by default unless explicitly configured via the zfs block; ZFS is not assumed/required
Snapshots are keyed by plan id so each plan's pre-mutation state is independently identifiable
When snapshots_required is false/unconfigured, execution proceeds without a snapshot
Pre-mutation snapshots are optional; the pipeline runs fully and safely without them
clauses.omit_reason: overlaps disabled-default; exercised by disabled/no-dataset tests, no dedicated runs-without-snapshots assertion

clauses.title: When snapshots_required is true, failure to take a required snapshot is fatal and execution aborts before mutating
A taken snapshot occurs after dependency-validation and lock acquisition but before the first mutation
On an initialized workspace, any root entry other than managed folders + control/dot dirs is a hard block; 0-sources is the only inbox
Structural guards are evaluated at each phase's startup after the lock and seal check, before any scan/plan/mutation
clauses.omit_reason: guards run at startup on happy paths; no dedicated assertion of ordering (after lock+seal, before scan)

clauses.title: The managed 0-6 structure is never silently rebuilt; a missing folder on an initialized workspace is a hard block, not auto-recreated
Symlinks are barred everywhere among managed files: never followed/organized, a hard blocker reporting the path with no plan/artifact produced
Base-is-folders invariant does not apply to an uninitialized workspace (first dump triggers init), except a root symlink is still barred
Manual GPS coords must be in-range and well-formed; manual UTC offsets must parse within sane bounds
Enumerated/structured decision values must be the right type/permitted value; a structurally broken decision is a validation error
Every human-authored value (config + decision fields) must be sanity-validated before use, each run at consumption
Validation fails closed before mutation: a failure is a hard blocker that produces no downstream artifact and mutates nothing
filename_timestamp_format must produce a non-empty filesystem-safe component with no path separators or illegal chars
A validation error names the offending file and field; validation never silently repairs a value
Threshold/numeric config (GPX distances/times, job counts) must be the right type and within sane ranges
Config paths must resolve to a sane valid path of the expected kind, contain no illegal chars, not escape bounds, not be empty
An invalid authored value is preserved and flagged for correction, not deleted/erased
Any user IANA timezone must be a real resolvable zone, not an arbitrary string
The ZFS snapshot name prefix/dataset names must be valid snapshot components; an invalid prefix is rejected before any snapshot
